stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
stimulus: A nowcast in meteorology attempts to describe weather conditions over the next zero to six ______ its forecasts are therefore much more immediate than those of a short-range forecast, which attempts to describe weather conditions for the following one to three days.
rationale:
Choice A is correct. A semicolon properly joins the two independent clauses ("A nowcast...hours" and "its forecasts...days").

Choice B creates a run-on; "and" without a preceding comma can't join two independent clauses here. Choice C creates a comma splice. Choice D creates a run-on with no punctuation or conjunction.
